label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to find out how long the experiment lasted. The question is asking about the duration of the experiment mentioned in the text. First, the passage states that the participants were asked to write for 20 minutes per day for three consecutive days. So the writing part of the experiment lasted three days. However, the experiment as a whole might have lasted longer because participants were monitored after the writing period to see how their wounds healed. The next relevant part is: "Two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a standard 4mm skin biopsy on their inner arm." So, two weeks (14 days) after the writing sessions, they had the biopsy. Then, the wounds were photographed regularly over the following days to determine the healing rate. The results mention that on the 11th day after the biopsy, some wounds were completely healed. That means the total duration from the start of the writing sessions to the point when the healing was measured is three days of writing + two weeks (14 days) until the biopsy + 11 days after the biopsy = 3 + 14 + 11 = 28 days, which is about a month. However, the experiment's duration is likely considered from the start of the writing sessions until the end of the monitoring period. The key point is that the participants were monitored for 11 days after the biopsy to track wound healing. So the full experiment duration would be the three days of writing plus the two weeks before the biopsy and the 11 days of monitoring after the biopsy, totaling about a month. The question is asking about the duration of the experiment. The answer options are (A) about three days, (B) about ten days, (C) about two weeks, and (D) about
